name: dev-workflows
description: General-purpose development workflow skills. Ships grill-then-plan (domain-aware grilling that sharpens terminology, captures decisions inline as CONTEXT.md/ADRs, then hands off to the superpowers planning pipeline), problem-description (generate interactive step-by-step HTML walkthroughs that explain a complex technical problem with concrete data), management-talk (reshape engineer-to-engineer updates for engineering-org leadership, shaped for the channel — JIRA, Slack, standup, email, or meeting talking-points), invoice-generator (turn git commits into a per-day work summary and always reshape it for leadership/the target channel via management-talk — Tribletext entry, Slack, standup, email, JIRA, or meeting talking-points), fit-gap-analysis (compare a spec/vision/competitor/RFP against a system as actually built — an evidence-first capability matrix + step-by-step user-journey comparison verified against the live system, rolled up into decisions), naming-audit (verify a source list of claimed labels/values/mappings against a system's authoritative source of record item-by-item — trace each label to its bound field, pull the live authoritative value, compare wording, source-of-truth wins; outputs a fixed per-item card with verdict and the exact app+code path to check), and study-design-verify (evidence-grounded advisory pipeline for 'how should this work?' questions about any real system — parallel study readers with structured citable findings, an independent design panel with conflicting value systems, and an adversarial feasibility reviewer that attacks every design against the live schema, code, and usage data before anything is recommended; outputs a phased advisory with an auditable evidence trail). More skills to come.
